meta.name: SAPS Short Almost Perfect Scale
meta.description: The SAPS is an 8-item brief scale measuring two core dimensions of perfectionism: Standards (high personal standards) and Discrepancy (perceived gap between standards and performance), helping distinguish adaptive from maladaptive perfectionism.
meta.author: Rice, Richardson & Tueller (2014)
meta.reference: Rice SP, Richardson CME, Tueller S. The Short Form of the Revised Almost Perfect Scale. Journal of Personality Assessment. 2014;96(3):368-379.
meta.instruction: Please rate how much you agree with each statement.

questions.text: I have high standards for my performance at work or at school
I expect the best from myself
I often feel frustrated because I can't meet my goals
My best just never seems to be good enough for me
I rarely live up to my high standards
I am never satisfied with my accomplishments

ranges.label: Non-perfectionist
ranges.description: Your perfectionism tendency is low, with relatively low personal standards. This indicates that this personality dimension falls within the typical range commonly seen in the general population.
suggestions: Appropriately raising your expectations can promote growth
Set specific and measurable goals
Understanding your personality traits supports personal growth
Every person has unique strengths and value in their personality
Flexibly apply your traits in different situations

ranges.label: Adaptive Perfectionist
ranges.description: You have high standards but low discrepancy, which is a healthy form of perfectionism. This indicates that this personality dimension falls within the typical range commonly seen in the general population.
suggestions: Continue maintaining the balance between high standards and self-acceptance
Be mindful of standards gradually becoming unrealistic
Understanding your personality traits supports personal growth
Every person has unique strengths and value in their personality
Flexibly apply your traits in different situations

ranges.label: Maladaptive Perfectionist
ranges.description: You not only have high standards but also frequently feel you cannot meet them, which may lead to anxiety, self-criticism, and procrastination. This trait is notably expressed and may influence your interpersonal interactions and self-perception in certain situations.
suggestions: Learn to distinguish between 'good enough' and 'perfect'
Practice self-compassion and reduce self-criticism
Focus on progress rather than perfection
If perfectionism significantly impacts your life, consider consulting a mental health professional
Gain deeper insight into how this trait affects your relationships

standards.label: Low Standards
standards.description: You have relatively low personal expectations. This level of trait expression is slightly above average but within the normal range of individual variation.
suggestions: Learn how this trait influences your behavior and relationships
Leverage the positive aspects of this trait
Learn adaptive coping strategies when needed
Communicate with others to foster mutual understanding

standards.label: Moderate Standards
standards.description: You have reasonable self-expectations. This indicates that this personality dimension falls within the typical range commonly seen in the general population.
suggestions: Understanding your personality traits supports personal growth
Every person has unique strengths and value in their personality
Flexibly apply your traits in different situations

standards.label: High Standards
standards.description: You have very high expectations and demands for yourself. This indicates that this personality dimension falls within the typical range commonly seen in the general population.
suggestions: Understanding your personality traits supports personal growth
Every person has unique strengths and value in their personality
Flexibly apply your traits in different situations

discrepancy.label: Low Discrepancy
discrepancy.description: You are generally able to accept your performance. This indicates that this personality dimension falls within the typical range commonly seen in the general population.
suggestions: Understanding your personality traits supports personal growth
Every person has unique strengths and value in their personality
Flexibly apply your traits in different situations

discrepancy.label: Moderate Discrepancy
discrepancy.description: You sometimes feel your performance is not good enough. This level of trait expression is slightly above average but within the normal range of individual variation.
suggestions: Learn how this trait influences your behavior and relationships
Leverage the positive aspects of this trait
Learn adaptive coping strategies when needed
Communicate with others to foster mutual understanding

discrepancy.label: High Discrepancy
discrepancy.description: You frequently feel you cannot meet your standards, which may cause emotional distress. This trait is notably expressed and may influence your interpersonal interactions and self-perception in certain situations.
suggestions: Gain deeper insight into how this trait affects your relationships
Learn to adjust your behavior in different situations
If the trait causes distress, consider consulting a professional
Develop self-awareness and emotional management skills
